channel.title: IBD LitMonitor - Biomarkers & Precision Medicine
channel.description: IBD literature monitoring: Biomarkers & Precision Medicine

item.title: APE1/Ref-1: multifunctional biology, selective inhibition, and the path to clinical translation.
item.description: Expert opinion on therapeutic targets - Review - Apurinic/apyrimidinic endonuclease 1/redox factor-1 (APE1/Ref-1) is a multifunctional stress-response regulator that coordinates genome maintenance, redox signaling, RNA biology, and cellular metab...

item.title: NOX1 Drives Inflammatory Bowel Disease Through Oxidant-Dependent Stabilization of IκBζ.
item.description: Free radical biology & medicine - NADPH oxidase 1 (NOX1), predominantly expressed in colonic epithelial cells, regulates mucosal immunity and homeostasis through the production of reactive oxygen species (ROS). However, its dysregu...

item.title: From Genetic Associations to Cellular Mechanisms: Resolving IBD Risk One Cell at a Time.
The Diagnostic Accuracy of Fecal Calprotectin for Colonoscopic Abnormalities in Patients With Chronic Diarrhea.
item.description: Journal of gastroenterology and hepatology - To evaluate the diagnostic accuracy of the colloidal gold immunochromatographic assay-based fecal calprotectin (CGICA-FC) for detecting colonoscopic abnormalities in patients with chronic diarrhea....

item.title: Lowering the C-Reactive Protein (CRP) Threshold Improves Risk Stratification in Acute Ulcerative Colitis: A Propensity-Matched Analysis.
item.description: Alimentary pharmacology & therapeutics - Real-world data demonstrate that patients hospitalised with non-acute severe UC (NASUC) experience intravenous corticosteroid (IVCS) failure rates approaching those of ASUC, yet no dedicated manage...

item.title: Trained immunity: An unexplored component of IBD pathogenesis.
item.description: Inflammatory bowel diseases - Trained immunity (TI) describes the capability of the innate immune system and its tissue-resident cells to acquire long-lasting functional adaptations following an initial inflammatory or microbia...

item.title: Meta-Analysis of DNA methylation and gut microbiome data in preterm birth reveals epigenetic and microbial biomarkers for early diagnosis and probiotic-based intervention.
item.description: Archives of physiology and biochemistry - Background: Globally, preterm birth continues to be a major contributor to neonatal morbidity and mortality. Developing early diagnostics and focused interventions requires an understanding of the ...

item.title: A microbiome meta-transcriptomics pipeline identifies a neutrophil elastase inhibitor that protects the colonic epithelial barrier.
item.description: Signal transduction and targeted therapy - Inflammatory Bowel Diseases (IBD) are lifelong conditions. Current therapeutic approaches target inflammatory signalling rather than improving barrier permeability or repair. The gut microbiome pro...

item.title: From signals to systems: the epigenetic-microbiome-mitochondrial axis in IBD pathogenesis.
item.description: Gut microbes - Review - Inflammatory bowel disease (IBD), including Crohn's disease and ulcerative colitis, is increasingly recognized not merely as an immune-mediated disorder, but as a systems-level condition arising fr...

item.title: Letter to the Editor to "Volatomics-based biomarkers for non-invasive diagnosis and monitoring of inflammatory bowel disease".
Hudi enteric-coated capsules promote colonic mucosa restoration via modulation of Wnt/β-catenin signaling in inflammatory bowel disease.
item.description: Fitoterapia - Hudi Enteric-coated Capsules (HD capsules), derived from a traditional enema formulation, are used clinically to treat inflammatory bowel disease (IBD). However, their bioactive components and unde...
